hard.next_step: Confidence 75% 3rd Jul- End of July customer will be concluded all evaluations for the competition from Demo point of view our position it was good and some tech queries need response from our team , additionally we need to provide revised quotes via KPMG and Accenture and KPMG as per customer request . 23rd of June 2026 - scheduled for live Demo SRM , and we will discuss our position with the RFP and will discuss SIs and DC . 6th Jun Ongoing clarifications with advisor (Franck) , references, DC, info Sec etc 21st May- Franck reached out to get clarification on "on prem vs cloud" response in our RFP- ck has responded 13th May - RFP Clarifications (~600) submitted and receipt clarification received .... considering the complexity expecting this will be an iterative process. 4th May- RFP submitted- clarification questions now received (extensive)- asked for extension to respond- accepted request.
hard.ais_why: AI is Zycus's chosen differentiator, not the buyer's stated criterion; 6 AI SKUs in scope on a Saudi defence/aerospace RFP shortlisted to top-3; ST Engineering (full Agentic suite customer) reference was pivotal in shortlisting; AI narrative is rep-driven ('drive a strong AI message', 26 Feb 2026).

forecast_read.reason: Best Case is not defensible on a still-open 3-vendor evaluation with no buyer-voiced preference, no champion, and the true budget holder above Franck Devienne unmapped. This is a date-and-number problem on an otherwise healthy, engaged deal: SAMI decides end of July 2026, and the 29 Aug 2026 close leaves only 4-6 weeks for SI-partner-mediated commercials, defence/InfoSec close-out and contracting on a $2.2M A&D deal.
forecast_read.math: 51 days to close as of 2026-07-09. In Shortlisted since the confidential down-select to 3 on 2 Mar 2026 (~129 days in stage). Close already slipped once: 29 Jun 2026 to 29 Aug 2026 on 7 Jun 2026. SAMI's stated timeline (23 Jun 2026 demo notes; 3 Jul 2026 Next Step): decision round mid-July, evaluations conclude end of July. Even a mid-July award leaves under 7 weeks for partner-routed negotiation plus defence compliance and contracting; realistic close is Oct-Nov 2026.

metrics.narrative: No buyer-quantified figures (cost, cycle time, savings) have surfaced on any call; the value case is qualitative. MEDDPICC 2.0 (2026-05-15) frames the problem as fragmented, manual supplier-facing processes across SAMI's HOLDCO/OPCO structure and the need for one auditable platform covering direct/defence procurement, localization, local content, SDP and IPP. The 23 Jun 2026 demo showed a Vision-2030 local-content dashboard (50% LC target) that resonated, but the quantification step has never been made a deliverable.

economic_buyer.narrative: MEDDPICC 2.0 (2026-05-15) names Franck Devienne, Senior Procurement Advisor, as economic buyer, with budget 'centrally held, but Franck and team will determine the award decision.' Franck is the consistent commercial gatekeeper: he ran the 23 Jun 2026 demo agenda, drove the on-prem/AEC hosting requirement (16 Jun 2026 email) and gates all vendor communication. But no CFO/CPO/board-level sponsor has appeared on any call, and the CRM itself flags 'a very arms-length process.' Treat Franck as the de facto commercial gatekeeper with true budget authority above him unconfirmed.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well documented and were tested live: functional/technical compliance, cybersecurity (on-prem constraint), SAP S/4HANA integration, A&D/direct-procurement/MRO fit, local content/LCGPA/SDP/IPP fit, KSA implementation and support capability, knowledge transfer, the filled compliance matrix and the commercial response (SF MEDDPICC_2_0__c, 2026-05-15). The 23 Jun 2026 demo drilled exactly these: eRFX with two-envelope bids and e-auctions, supplier onboarding with GAMI/iCV/GOSI fields, the local-content dashboard, classified-material catalog handling (Junaid's question) and S/4HANA master-data integration.

decision_process.narrative: A clean, traceable tender sequence: NDA (15 Dec 2025), RFI (received 16 Dec 2025, submitted 24 Jan 2026), Innovation Contest presentations (22-26 Feb 2026), confidential down-select to 3 vendors (2 Mar 2026), RFP issued 13 Mar 2026 and extended to 21 Apr 2026 with EY agreeing to prime with 30% local Saudi resources, RFP submitted 4 May 2026, ~600 clarifications answered 13 May 2026, live SRM demo 23 Jun 2026. SAMI stated a final decision round around mid-July and evaluation conclusion by end of July 2026 (23 Jun demo notes; 3 Jul 2026 Next Step). SAMI will select both the SRM solution and the implementor, concurrent with its SAP rollout where Accenture and KPMG are already primed (Franck email, 19 Jun 2026).

paper_process.narrative: No MSA/SOW, legal or formal InfoSec-review artifacts have surfaced; the deal is still in evaluation. Two paper-shaping facts are already known: SAMI requires revised commercial quotes routed via SI partners KPMG and Accenture (3 Jul 2026 Next Step; Franck email 19 Jun 2026), and hosting must be on-premise at AEC, a SAMI defence subsidiary (Franck email, 16 Jun 2026), which adds a data-centre and cybersecurity dimension to any contract. The contracting vehicle (direct MSA vs SI-mediated framework) has not been asked and is the visibility move.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well articulated in MEDDPICC 2.0 (2026-05-15): fragmented, manual supplier-facing processes spanning onboarding, qualification, sourcing and development; no standardized supplier data across the HOLDCO/OPCO structure; and the need to run direct/defence procurement, localization, local content, SDP and IPP on one auditable platform. The 23 Jun 2026 demo confirmed the specifics live: buyers probed classified-material catalog governance, GAMI oversight workflows, cross-functional supplier-registration approvals and local-content calculation, all pains of the current manual model. The pain owner is SAMI procurement (Franck Devienne's team).

champion.narrative: No champion is identified. MEDDPICC 2.0 (2026-05-15) is explicit: 'Very arms-length process - Carl has had positive conversations with Franck, but he doesn't fit the champion role.' Franck Devienne has been consistently present (21 May 2026 clarification, 23 Jun 2026 demo host, June-July email threads) but acts as a neutral process gatekeeper enforcing equal vendor treatment, not an internal advocate. Yousef Alarabi is actively engaged (chased the final proposal on 2 and 7 Jul 2026) but chasing deliverables is process diligence, not advocacy. No internal introduction or pro-Zycus push has been evidenced on any call.

competition.narrative: Four named rivals (Competitors__c; MEDDPICC 2.0, 2026-05-15): SAP Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, Coupa. SAP Ariba is the strongest current threat: SAMI runs S/4HANA, its SAP rollout is already primed by Accenture and KPMG (Franck email, 19 Jun 2026), and the buyer probes vendors on 'why not Ariba' given the SAP/IBM tie, though the same CRM note reads 'clearly they are unliked.' All shortlisted vendors demoed the same agenda the week of 23 Jun 2026 (Franck, demo transcript). Zycus's wedge is the ST Engineering A&D reference (pivotal in shortlisting, 13 Jan 2026), the local-content dashboard shown 23 Jun 2026, and the deeper AI story; the open flank is Saudi/S-4HANA integration proof, still owed with the A&D customer-list clarifications (Franck, 4-10 Jun 2026).

critical_signals.text: SAMI is waiting on Zycus's final proposal and the KPMG/Accenture implementation quotes; Yousef Alarabi chased on 2 Jul and again on 7 Jul 2026, right as the end-July decision window opens.

The 23 Jun 2026 live SRM demo ran 2.5 hours with SAMI's evaluation, localization and MRO teams probing deep (classified catalogs, GAMI workflows, local-content dashboards), and the rep's 3 Jul note records Zycus's demo position as good.
SAP Ariba rides SAMI's S/4HANA estate and its Accenture/KPMG-primed SAP rollout; the Saudi S/4HANA integration proof SAMI asked for on 4 Jun 2026 is still not fully closed.

SAMI confirmed on 16 Jun 2026 that hosting must be on-premise at AEC (its defence subsidiary), and its cybersecurity posture blocks external API validations, reshaping the solution and commercial architecture.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is one of three finalists in SAMI's $2.2M full-S2P tender with a decision due end of July 2026. The buyer is actively pulling (chased the final proposal twice in the first week of July), the 23 Jun demo landed well, and the ST Engineering A&D reference anchors credibility. What holds the position back is structural: no champion, no engaged economic buyer above Franck Devienne, no buyer-voiced preference, and two Zycus-owed deliverables (final SI-routed quotes, demo tech-query responses) outstanding since 23 Jun.

win_position.text: Shortlisted to a confidential final 3 (SAMI confirmed 2 Mar 2026) in a live full-S2P RFP, with the 23 Jun 2026 demo position recorded as good in the 3 Jul rep note.

The ST Engineering reference (a Zycus A&D customer on the full Agentic suite) was pivotal in shortlisting (13 Jan 2026) and remains the defence-credibility anchor Franck keeps probing (A&D OEM list requests, 4-10 Jun 2026).

Commercial topics are live: SAMI requested revised quotes via KPMG and Accenture (19 Jun-3 Jul 2026) and is chasing the final proposal (Yousef Alarabi, 7 Jul 2026), a buyer that wants your number is a buyer still considering you.

No champion and no buyer-voiced preference: Franck Devienne runs an explicitly arms-length process (MEDDPICC 2.0, 15 May 2026) and enforces identical treatment of all three vendors; nobody inside SAMI is fighting for Zycus.
SAP Ariba's structural advantage is real: SAMI runs S/4HANA and its SAP rollout is primed by the same SIs (Accenture, KPMG) SAMI wants Zycus to quote through (Franck, 19 Jun 2026).

deal_momentum.text: Buyer-initiated contact twice in the last 7 days: Yousef Alarabi chased the vendor proposals on 2 Jul and the final proposal on 7 Jul 2026.
A live, dated buyer process: decision round mid-July, all evaluations concluded end of July 2026 (23 Jun demo notes; 3 Jul Next Step).
The 23 Jun 2026 live SRM demo was a 2.5-hour buyer-hosted working session with SAMI's evaluation, localization and MRO teams, recorded by Franck for the wider committee.
The ball is in Zycus's court: demo tech-query responses and SI-partner revised quotes have been outstanding since 23 Jun 2026 (~16 days), and the buyer is now chasing.

customer_commitment.text: SAMI invited Zycus to the SRM demo round on its own initiative (16 Jun 2026) and committed a 2.5-hour committee session on 23 Jun 2026.
SAMI set and shared its own decision timeline (mid-July decision round, end-July evaluation conclusion) and is chasing Zycus deliverables against it (2 and 7 Jul 2026).
All engagement flows through the procurement evaluation team (Franck, Yousef, Ahmed); no executive or budget-holder above them has engaged with Zycus at any point.

deal_risk.text: Timeline: close 29 Aug 2026 already slipped once (from 29 Jun, on 7 Jun 2026); an end-July award leaves 4-6 weeks for SI-mediated commercials, defence InfoSec and contracting on a $2.2M A&D deal.

Partner dependency: SAMI wants implementation quotes via Accenture and KPMG; Carl noted on 19 Jun 2026 'we will try to encourage ACC to propose with us', so one of the two required SI paths is not yet secured.
Solution architecture: on-premise hosting at AEC (confirmed 16 Jun 2026) with cybersecurity blocking external API validation narrows Zycus's SaaS-native strengths and raises delivery risk.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer-voiced preference. The 3 Jul 2026 Next Step records 'from Demo point of view our position it was good' as relayed feedback, but Franck runs an explicitly equal three-vendor process (same demo agenda to all vendors, 23 Jun 2026 transcript) and the CRM notes SAMI probes all vendors on 'why not Ariba'.

business_case.evidence: Pain is confirmed and specific (fragmented supplier processes, local-content/GAMI compliance, MEDDPICC 2.0, 15 May 2026) but no buyer-quantified value figures exist; the local-content dashboard shown 23 Jun 2026 is the closest thing to a value artifact.

deal_movement.summary: The deal moved through its use-case demo gate and into the commercial run-up: SAMI hosted the live SRM demo on 23 Jun 2026, demanded SI-routed quotes and an AEC-hosted proposal through late June, and chased Zycus's final proposal twice in early July. Close date slipped 29 Jun to 29 Aug on 7 Jun 2026 and deal ownership consolidated under Mohamad Alhakim (Riyadh) on 1 Jul 2026.

items.change: Buyer chased Zycus's final proposal ('We are still waiting for your final proposal', Yousef Alarabi)
Zycus sent the revised DC option (AEC data-warehouse, slide 5) to SAMI
Co-owner changed from Dan Quinn to Mohamad Alhakim
Live SRM demo delivered to SAMI's evaluation committee (2.5 hours, recorded by the buyer)
SAMI confirmed AEC (its subsidiary) hosting counts as on-premise; revised proposal required

day_summary.overall: SAMI kept the pressure on Zycus's commercial deliverables. Yousef Alarabi wrote that SAMI is still waiting for Zycus's final proposal, and Carl Kimball replied that the SI implementation proposals with KPMG and EY are nearly complete and the updated proposal list had already been sent. The exchange confirms the evaluation channel is live and the buyer is driving toward its end-July decision.

items.name: SAMI chases the final proposal
items.summary: Yousef Alarabi (SAMI) wrote that SAMI is still waiting for Zycus's final proposal.

items.name: Zycus responds on SI proposals
items.summary: Carl Kimball replied that the KPMG and EY SI proposals are nearly complete and the updated proposal list was already sent.

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the single strongest current threat: SAMI runs S/4HANA, its SAP OPCO rollout is primed by Accenture and KPMG (Franck email, 19 Jun 2026), and the buyer probes vendors on 'why not Ariba' given the SAP/IBM ecosystem tie, though the CRM notes Ariba is 'clearly unliked.' All three shortlisted vendors demoed the identical agenda the week of 23 Jun 2026, so the field is a genuine bake-off. GEP (services-heavy, natural for a complex A&D rollout) and Ivalua (heavy customisation/localisation relevant to KSA local content) are live secondary threats with no fresh 2026 evidence changing their standing. Coupa is the lowest current threat, cited for its A&D customer base with no fresh 2026 signal at SAMI.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Ariba (relationship with SAP/IBM - but they ask for detail on why nor Ariba, clearly they are unliked)
competitors.source: SF MEDDPICC_2_0__c (2026-05-15); Franck email 2026-06-19 (Accenture/KPMG priming the SAP rollout)
competitors.how_we_win: Deliver the Saudi/S-4HANA integration proof points (iSaaS adapters, named A&D implementations from the 4-10 Jun 2026 customer-list exchange) with the final proposal before SAMI's end-July decision, removing the default-to-Ariba-as-lower-risk fallback.

competitors.name: Ivalua
competitors.how_we_win: Position the configurable-not-customized local-content dashboard demonstrated on 23 Jun 2026 as a lower-risk localization path than heavy customization.

competitors.name: GEP
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with the single-platform story plus the ST Engineering A&D reference (13 Jan 2026) to counter GEP's services-led pitch, and lock the Accenture/KPMG SI quotes so SAMI gets services depth with Zycus software.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: No fresh 2026 evidence of active Coupa traction at SAMI; monitor for resurgence but do not over-invest here.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus is aligned on the functional SRM scope and the local-content/GAMI compliance layer that SAMI probed hardest at the demo, partially aligned on the on-prem/AEC hosting architecture and defence-grade catalog governance where cybersecurity constraints limit standard SaaS patterns, and exposed on Saudi/S-4HANA integration proof until the requested A&D implementation evidence lands.

best_practice_check.summary: The deal is in a formal three-way bake-off with a dated buyer decision window and Zycus holding the ball on two deliverables. The win levers now are speed on the owed commercials, credibility proof against Ariba's SAP-ecosystem pull, and building any internal advocate before the committee decides.
flags: Deliver before the buyer chases a third time: the final AEC-hosted proposal and the KPMG/Accenture SI quotes are the current stage-gate; every chase email (2 Jul, 7 Jul) erodes execution credibility in a process where all three vendors are graded identically.

Break the single-thread, arms-length pattern: all Zycus contact runs through the procurement evaluation trio (Franck, Yousef, Ahmed); use Mohamad Alhakim's Riyadh presence to build a genuine internal advocate and reach the budget holder before the mid-July decision round.

Close the Ariba flank with proof, not claims: land the Saudi/S-4HANA integration evidence and the requested A&D reference depth (ST Engineering, Curtiss-Wright, DefTech detail Franck asked for on 4 Jun) inside the final proposal.

No SAMI stakeholder has evidenced internal advocacy for Zycus on any call or email through 7 Jul 2026.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is Zycus's chosen edge, not the buyer's stated criterion: the 26 Feb 2026 rep note says the team is 'investigating how we can drive a strong AI message' because all three finalists are similar fits. Six AI SKUs are in scope and the ST Engineering Agentic-suite reference was pivotal in shortlisting, but at the 23 Jun demo Franck reacted positively when told the search feature did NOT require AI ('Good. Thanks.'), a signal to position AI as optional value, not a dependency.

ai_fit_signal.summary: SAMI evaluates on functional compliance, cybersecurity and localization first; AI is welcomed as differentiation (Merlin Intake teased at the demo) but the buyer showed relief that core functions work without AI (Franck, 23 Jun 2026 demo). A defence buyer with on-prem constraints will scrutinize AI architecture.

On-premise hosting at AEC with SAMI cybersecurity blocking external API validation (stated at the 23 Jun demo: 'our cybersecurity... it cannot be implemented') constrains Zycus's SaaS-native third-party checks and required a reworked proposal.

The deal is not cooling; the risk is Zycus's own delivery pace into the decision window.

drivers: ✅ Shortlisted to a confidential final 3 (SAMI confirmed 2 Mar 2026) in a live $2.2M full-S2P RFP, with the 23 Jun 2026 demo position recorded as good and evaluations concluding end of July.
✅ The buyer is pulling: SAMI chased Zycus's vendor proposals on 2 Jul and its final proposal on 7 Jul 2026 — the committee wants Zycus's number before it decides.
✅ The ST Engineering A&D reference was pivotal in shortlisting (13 Jan 2026) and anchors Zycus's defence credibility against three suite rivals.

⚠️ No champion: Franck Devienne runs an explicitly arms-length, equal-vendor process (MEDDPICC 2.0, 15 May 2026) ► have Mohamad Alhakim build an in-person advocate in Riyadh before the mid-July decision round.
⚠️ The economic buyer above Franck is unmapped; budget is centrally held ► ask Franck directly who approves the award and secure exec contact before end-July.
⚠️ SAP Ariba rides SAMI's S/4HANA estate and its Accenture/KPMG-primed SAP rollout ► ship the Saudi/S-4HANA integration proof and A&D reference depth inside the final proposal.
✅ Buyer-initiated contact twice in the last 7 days: Yousef Alarabi chased the vendor proposals (2 Jul) and the final proposal (7 Jul 2026).
✅ Live process with dated buyer milestones: final decision round mid-July, all evaluations concluded end of July 2026 (23 Jun demo notes; 3 Jul Next Step).
✅ The 23 Jun 2026 live SRM demo was a 2.5-hour buyer-hosted committee session, recorded by Franck for the wider evaluation team.
✅ Deliverables are landing: revised DC option sent 2 Jul, A&D customer evidence 4-10 Jun, AEC hosting resolved 16-22 Jun — the clarification cadence is two-way and dense.
⚠️ Zycus holds the ball: the final proposal and SI-partner quotes have been owed since ~23 Jun (~16 days) and the buyer is now chasing ► land both this week, before a third chase.
⚠️ Close date already pushed once (29 Jun → 29 Aug, on 7 Jun 2026) ► re-baseline the close against decision-plus-contracting lead time when the award lands.
